headline: ServiceNow stock gains as investors push further into software
summary: ServiceNow (NOW) shares are extending their gains in this trading week, jumping by nearly 5% on Tuesday morning in a new push by software stocks. Yahoo Finance Senior Reporter Ines Ferré takes a closer look at why investors may be rotating out of semiconductor stocks into software. AI semiconductor giant Nvidia (NVDA) is scheduled to report first quarter earnings this Wednesday, May 20.

content.title: ServiceNow stock gains as investors push further into software

AI semiconductor giant Nvidia (NVDA) is scheduled to report first quarter earnings this Wednesday, May 20.

headline: US software stocks attempt a rebound as investors reassess AI risks
summary: U.S. software stocks headed toward their fourth consecutive session of gains on Tuesday, attempting a comeback after being battered ‌for much of the year on fears of disruption from artificial intelligence.  The iShares Expanded Tech‑Software Sector ETF rose 1.1%, hitting its highest level since January.  Workday, ServiceNow and Salesforce ‌rose between 3.7% and ⁠4.3%.

headline: Workday to keep expanding Indian workforce, deepen AI investments, executive says
summary: Workday would sustain the pace of workforce expansion in India, a top ‌executive said on Tuesday, as the human resources and enterprise ‌software maker deepens investments in artificial intelligence and opens new offices.  India is home to more ​than half of all global capability centres as companies prefer its large skilled workforce, lower operating costs and rising ability to support high-value jobs across technology, finance and engineering.  Workday has offices in financial capital Mumbai and two more ‌cities with plans to expand ⁠to Delhi and Bengaluru.

content.title: Workday to keep expanding Indian workforce, deepen AI investments, executive says

Workday has offices in financial capital Mumbai and two more ‌cities with plans to expand ⁠to Delhi and Bengaluru.

headline: US software stocks rebound, seeking to loosen AI's grip
summary: Shares of several U.S. software stocks gained on Tuesday, as the industry attempts a comeback after being battered for much of ‌the year on fears of disruption from artificial intelligence.  The iShares Expanded Tech-Software Sector ETF hit its highest level since January before paring gains, while Workday, ServiceNow and Salesforce rose between 1.4% and 2.4%.  The gains hint at a possible shift in investor sentiment as markets reassess software stocks following a painful valuation ⁠reset.

content.title: US software stocks rebound, seeking to loosen AI's grip
content.summary: Shares of several U.S. software stocks gained on Tuesday, as the industry attempts a comeback after being battered for much of ‌the year on fears of disruption from artificial intelligence.  The iShares Expanded Tech-Software Sector ETF hit its highest level since January before paring gains, while Workday, ServiceNow and Salesforce rose between 1.4% and 2.4%.  The gains hint at a possible shift in investor sentiment as markets reassess software stocks following a painful valuation ⁠reset.
